origin: harvested 2026-06-28 from a marketing content audit -- a comparative 'industry-leading' metric claim shipped with no cited source
artifact: A marketing review report on a landing-page headline reads: 'Claims-check: PASS, all figures supported.' The headline being graded reads: 'Customers see an industry-leading 3x faster results than any competitor.' The report's evidence section lists only one internal source: a single anonymized customer testimonial that says 'it felt faster'. No measurement, no benchmark study, and no competitor comparison data appear anywhere in the cited evidence.
rubric: A correct verifier must catch that a quantified comparative superiority claim ('industry-leading 3x faster ... than any competitor') is certified PASS while the only cited evidence is one qualitative testimonial ('it felt faster') with no measurement and no competitor benchmark -- the '3x' and 'industry-leading' are unsubstantiated. PASS only if the verifier rejects the supported-figures claim for lack of a real source rather than accepting the confident headline.
